seo_description: Current time in Nairobi, Kenya. East Africa Time (EAT/UTC+3) year-round. No daylight saving. Time differences, business hours & world clock.

faq.question: What time is it in Nairobi right now?
faq.answer: Nairobi operates on East Africa Time (EAT/UTC+3) year-round. Kenya does not observe daylight saving time, so Nairobi maintains a consistent UTC+3 offset throughout all seasons. This makes Nairobi 8 hours ahead of New York (EST), 3 hours ahead of London (GMT), and 6 hours behind Tokyo (JST). The fixed timezone simplifies international coordination with Kenya's capital and largest city, home to approximately 5 million people in the metropolitan area.

faq.question: What time zone is Nairobi in?
faq.answer: Nairobi uses East Africa Time (EAT), permanently set at UTC+3 throughout the entire year. This timezone is shared with other East African capitals including Addis Ababa (Ethiopia), Dar es Salaam (Tanzania), and Kampala (Uganda). EAT also aligns with Moscow Standard Time (MSK) and Arabia Standard Time (AST) at UTC+3, placing Nairobi in convenient overlap with both Middle Eastern and European business hours. As Africa's leading tech hub — often called 'Silicon Savannah' — Nairobi's timezone supports its role as a gateway for international business across the continent.

faq.question: Does Nairobi observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Kenya does not observe daylight saving time, and Nairobi remains on East Africa Time (EAT/UTC+3) year-round. Because Nairobi sits near the equator at approximately 1.3° South latitude, day length barely changes throughout the year — sunrise and sunset occur close to 6:30 AM and 6:30 PM respectively in every season. This near-constant daylight pattern eliminates the practical need for DST clock adjustments. The permanent UTC+3 offset makes Nairobi highly predictable for international scheduling.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Nairobi and London?
faq.answer: Nairobi is 3 hours ahead of London during Greenwich Mean Time (GMT), and 2 hours ahead during British Summer Time (BST). When it's 9:00 AM in London, it's 12:00 PM (noon) in Nairobi during GMT, or 11:00 AM during BST. Since Nairobi does not observe DST, the time difference shifts when the UK changes clocks in late March and late October. This close time gap makes Nairobi-London business coordination straightforward, which is significant given Kenya's historical ties with the UK and strong bilateral trade. Check /time/nairobi/london/ for current time conversions.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Nairobi and New York?
faq.answer: Nairobi is 8 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (EST), and 7 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (EDT). When it's 9:00 AM in New York, it's 5:00 PM in Nairobi (EST) or 4:00 PM (EDT). Since Kenya does not observe DST, the time difference shifts twice yearly when the US changes clocks in March and November. The substantial gap means real-time business overlap is limited to Nairobi's late afternoon and New York's early morning. Visit /time/nairobi/new-york/ for precise current offset calculations.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Nairobi and Dubai?
faq.answer: Nairobi is only 1 hour behind Dubai, making it one of the closest major city pairs in terms of time offset. Dubai operates on Gulf Standard Time (GST/UTC+4), while Nairobi runs on EAT (UTC+3). When it's 10:00 AM in Dubai, it's 9:00 AM in Nairobi. Neither city observes daylight saving time, so this 1-hour difference remains constant year-round. This tight alignment facilitates strong business ties between East Africa and the Gulf states, with many direct flights connecting the two cities in under 5 hours.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Nairobi?
faq.answer: Nairobi businesses generally operate Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM EAT, with a lunch break often observed between 1:00 PM and 2:00 PM. Government offices typically run 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, while banks open 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM on weekdays and 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM on Saturdays. The Nairobi Securities Exchange (NSE) trades from 9:30 AM to 3:00 PM EAT, Monday through Friday. Nairobi's thriving tech sector and startup ecosystem often operate on more flexible schedules, with many international companies extending hours to overlap with European markets (opening at 6:00 AM - 8:00 AM GMT). Use /meeting/ to identify optimal collaboration windows between Nairobi and your timezone.

faq.question: Why does Nairobi have nearly equal daylight year-round?
faq.answer: Nairobi sits at approximately 1.3° South of the equator, placing it almost exactly on the Earth's equatorial line. This geographic position means the sun's path across the sky varies only minimally between seasons, resulting in roughly 12 hours of daylight and 12 hours of darkness every day of the year. Sunrise in Nairobi consistently occurs around 6:20-6:40 AM and sunset around 6:25-6:45 PM, with less than 30 minutes of variation across the entire year. This is dramatically different from cities like London or New York, where daylight hours can range from 8 to 16 hours depending on the season. The near-constant daylight pattern is one reason Kenya has never adopted daylight saving time.
